summary: Phase I+ Sent Reports Dashboard + Reminder endpoints end-to-end tested. Backend: 14/14 pytest tests pass covering list+counters, all 4 filters, sender scoping, reminder happy path with counter increment, 409 for non-pending, 404 for non-owner, 400 for >1000 char note, and in-app notification dispatch to recipient. Frontend: login → /digital-twin → Răspunsuri button (with badge=3) → fullscreen dashboard → all 5 filter pills render → filter-pending shows 3 rows, filter-confirmed shows 1 row with inline italic decision comment, filter-overdue shows 0 (empty state OK) → reminder modal opens with pin/project/age context → note input works → send → reminder-toast appears → modal closes → reminder_count incremented (2→3) and list refreshed. Status pill colors verified: status-pending=amber, status-confirmed=emerald, status-needs_changes=blue.

issues: UX combinator issue: data-testid=filter-overdue is a SEPARATE toggle from status filter pills (filter-all/pending/confirmed/needs_changes). After clicking filter-overdue (overdueOnly=true) then clicking filter-all, the overdueOnly state persists, causing the query to be ?overdue_only=true with no status, returning 0 items even though the 'Toate (5)' pill appears selected. List shows empty-state 'Niciun raport în această categorie' which is confusing because the counter says 5. Recommend: clicking any status pill should setOverdueOnly(false), OR keep current behavior but visually de-emphasize the status pill selection while overdue is on.

action_items: (OPTIONAL UX polish) In SentReportsDashboard.jsx, reset overdueOnly to false when user clicks any of the status filter pills (filter-all/pending/confirmed/needs_changes) so the dual-filter combination cannot silently hide all results.
critical_code_review_comments: SentReportsDashboard.jsx: filter state model uses two independent variables (filter + overdueOnly) which produces a confusing intersect-empty state when both are active. Consider unifying into a single 'view' enum (all|pending|confirmed|needs_changes|overdue) or auto-clear overdueOnly when filter changes.

Reminder button visibility is gated on `it.approval_status === 'pending' && it.approval_url` — correctly hides legacy reports without approval_url to avoid the documented 500 response. Good defensive UI.

Backend endpoint behavior fully matches spec including aggregation $unwind on report_history, sender_id scoping, in-app dt_report_reminder notification dispatch, and 409/404/400 error semantics with Romanian messages.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on required flows (1 minor UX issue)

test_credentials: admin@propmanage.io / Admin123!, client@propmanage.io / Client123!, specialist@propmanage.io / Spec123! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures create a dedicated 'TEST_PhaseIplus_SentReports' project + pin and 3 reports per module run (1 pending, 1 confirmed, 1 needs_changes). Project is cleaned up via module-scoped fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend Phase I+ Sent Reports endpoints (/api/digital-twin/reports/sent and /api/digital-twin/reports/{id}/remind) are fully covered by pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_phase_i_plus_sent_reports.py — re-run with `pytest tests/test_phase_i_plus_sent_reports.py -v`. Phase I approval tests at /app/backend/tests/test_phase_i_approval.py (14 tests) also still green. The reminder button on the row only renders when approval_status='pending' AND approval_url exists; legacy reports without approval_url correctly hide it (matches main-agent context). One UX bug worth fixing: filter-overdue is an independent toggle that combines with status filter (AND).
rca of the issue: Filter-overdue UX bug RCA — In /app/frontend/src/components/SentReportsDashboard.jsx, the dashboard uses two separate state vars: `filter` (default 'pending') and `overdueOnly` (default false). The overdue pill at line 187 toggles `overdueOnly` without affecting `filter`. The load() function (line 117-132) intersects both via querystring (status + overdue_only). Repro: open dashboard → click filter-overdue (0 results, OK) → click filter-all → list stays empty because query is ?overdue_only=true with no status, returning only overdue items (still 0). Mitigation: in onClick of status pills (line 175), also call `setOverdueOnly(false)`; alternatively merge overdue into the `filter` enum as a fifth exclusive value.
